data.title: Usiminas targets Q1 2025 domestic steel sales growth and projects 2.5% GDP growth for Brazil
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Usinas Siderúrgicas de Minas Gerais S.A. (OTCPK:USNZY) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Marcelo Chara, CEO, highlighted operational advances in 2024, achieving the second-largest crude steel production volume since 2015, with an 11% reduction in cost of sales per ton. The adjusted consolidated EBITDA reached BRL2.6 billion, with the steel unit achieving a 31% EBITDA growth year-over-year. He emphasized investments in repairing the coke and PCI plants, which are expected to reduce fuel costs and CO2 emissions.
  • Chara noted a 9.8% increase in flat steel demand to 15.7 million tons in 2024, driven by strong performance in the automotive, home appliances, and industrial sectors. He underscored the urgency of trade defense measures to combat unfair competition from subsidized imports, particularly from China.
  • Thiago Rodrigues, CFO, reported a net leverage ratio of 0.58 times at year-end 2024 and highlighted a $500 million debt issuance completed in January 2025 to extend debt maturities. Domestic steel sales rose 8% year-over-year, offsetting declines in mining production and sales.
  • The company’s Q4 2024 adjusted EBITDA grew 22% quarter-over-quarter to BRL528 million, supported by improved sales volumes in the steel segment.

Outlook

  • For Q1 2025, the company expects increased domestic steel sales, a better product mix, and slightly improved net revenue per ton due to price transfers in industrial contracts. Sales costs are projected to decrease further through continuous operational efficiency gains.
  • Chara projected a 2.5% GDP growth for Brazil in 2025, supported by sectors like home appliances, automobiles, and household essentials, while cautioning that high interest rates may slow growth.
  • Mining unit sales are anticipated to remain stable, with consolidated improvements expected by Q3 2025.

Financial Results

  • Domestic steel sales increased by 8% year-over-year, while total steel sales grew by 6%. Cost of goods sold dropped 11%, significantly boosting results despite weaker market prices. Adjusted EBITDA for the steel segment was BRL366 million for Q4 2024, with a stable margin of 6.3%.
  • Mining revenues remained stable at BRL767 million for Q4 2024 despite lower sales volumes, supported by favorable iron ore pricing and reduced freight costs. The unit’s EBITDA reached BRL154 million, driven by a 14 percentage point margin increase.
  • The company ended 2024 with a cash balance of BRL6 billion and a CapEx of BRL1.1 billion, aligned with guidance. CapEx for 2025 is projected between BRL1.4 billion and BRL1.6 billion, focusing on sustainability and operational improvements.

Q&A

  • Daniel Sasson, Itaú BBA, asked about cost reductions and efficiency gains. CFO Thiago Rodrigues explained that Q1 2025 cost reductions will primarily stem from operational efficiencies rather than significant exchange rate impacts.
  • Carlos de Alba, Morgan Stanley, inquired about working capital. Rodrigues clarified that working capital levels remain stable and are tied to sales volume fluctuations, with no major structural changes expected in 2025.
  • Miguel Homes, Vice President of Sales, addressed export strategies, noting a focus on serving the domestic market and regional exports, particularly to Argentina. Export volumes to non-regional markets are expected to decline in Q1 2025, with a better product mix potentially improving average prices.
  • Analysts raised concerns about CapEx delays. Rodrigues attributed these to the complexity of repairing the coke plant while maintaining operations, emphasizing that all approved investments are on track.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed slight concerns regarding cost pressures, macroeconomic uncertainties, and CapEx delays. Their tone was slightly negative, emphasizing challenges in global trade and domestic market conditions.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, highlighting the success of operational efficiencies, strategic investments, and resilience in addressing external challenges. CEO Marcelo Chara emphasized the company’s preparedness to navigate fluctuating market conditions.
  • During the Q&A, management adopted a more defensive tone, particularly on trade defense measures and macroeconomic risks, while reiterating confidence in long-term strategic initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Without the previous quarter's transcript, direct comparisons are unavailable. However, management reiterated its focus on operational efficiency gains, trade defense measures, and improving cost structures.
  • Analysts shifted their focus toward uncertainties in macroeconomic conditions and their potential impact on demand, reflecting cautious sentiment for 2025.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted challenges from subsidized imports, particularly from China, which have disrupted the domestic market. Chara emphasized the need for stronger trade defense measures to protect local industries.
  • Analysts raised concerns about high interest rates, macroeconomic slowdowns, and sustaining recent cost reductions.
  • Delays in key projects, including coke plant repairs and PCI plant upgrades, were noted but attributed to operational complexities.

Final Takeaway

Usiminas ended 2024 with strong operational efficiency gains and cost reductions, despite challenges from global market dynamics and unfair competition. Management remains optimistic about domestic steel sales growth in 2025, supported by strategic investments and trade defense advocacy. However, macroeconomic uncertainties and high interest rates pose potential risks to growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Phathom spikes as Cantor Fitzgerald bullish on IP claims
data.description:
Phathom Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:PHAT) traded higher on Friday after Cantor Fitzgerald initiated its coverage with an overweight recommendation despite a recent selloff attributed to the company's IP rights-related risk.

Cantor analyst Kristen Kluska argued that since early November, Phathom (NASDAQ:PHAT) had lost 67% compared to the 13% decline in the broader biotech space as represented by the SPDR S&P Biotech ETF (XBI).

Kluska noted that “concerns around Phathom's intellectual property,” related to its heartburn medication Voquezna (vonoprazan) have led to the selloff and added, “We acknowledge this remains a risk on the stock.”

However, the analyst argued that in her base case, assuming a patent term extension to 2030 and only a 50% probability of success for market exclusivity in 2031-2032, the stock “still gives significant upside from the current valuation.”

“Our checks support that at minimum, PHAT has strong formulation and compound patents that provide protection into 2030, something we believe the Street is missing," Kluska wrote with a $23 per share target on the stock.

data.title: Barrick Gold considers redomiciling to U.S., CEO Bristow says
data.description:
Barrick Gold (NYSE:GOLD) is weighing a relocation of its corporate domicile from Canada to the U.S., as a move could attract a broader base of investors, CEO Mark Bristow told The Globe and Mail on Friday.

A relocation also could position Barrick (NYSE:GOLD) for inclusion in the S&P 500, which would trigger automatic purchases from many mutual funds and ETFs that track the index, the CEO said.

Barrick (GOLD) has a strong operational presence in the U.S.: The Nevada Gold Mines joint venture with Newmont is the company's largest operation and the world's biggest gold-producing complex, expected to produce up to 1.7M attributable gold oz this year, accounting for nearly half of Barrick’s total output; the Goldrush project in Nevada, which is anticipated to produce more than 400K oz/year by 2028; and the Fourmile gold project, also in Nevada.

Bristow also suggested the Trump administration could facilitate a move to the U.S. under its “America First” agenda.

Tax regulations make relocating to the U.S. is a complicated and costly process, so a faster alternative could be acquiring a major U.S. company, but Bristow says there are few attractive acquisition targets.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: Apple product event, Walmart earnings, Sandisk debut, and Visa's Investor Day
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

Monday - February 17

  • All day - The U.S. markets will be closed for observance of the President's Day holiday.
  • All day - The Chicago Auto Show will wrap up with interactive elements and activation events, including test drives. Electric vehicle makers will have a heavy presence at the auto show, including Polestar (PSNY) with the Polestar 3, Rivian Automotive (RIVN) with the R1T and R1 models, VinFast Automotive (VFS) with the VF8, Volkswagen (OTCPK:VWAGY) with the ID.4 and ID.Buzz models, and a rare auto show appearance for Tesla (TSLA) with the Cybertruck on display.

Tuesday - February 18

  • Volatility watch - MicroCloud Hologram (HOLO) and Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (FFIE) have a high level of implied volatility based on options trading. Medical Properties Trust (MPW) and Kohl's (KSS) head into the new week with a high level of short interest outstanding on them. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Firefly Neuroscience (AIFF), Anteris Technologies Global (AVR), and Triumph Group (TGI). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Venture Global (VG), Columbus McKinnon (CMCO), and Brazil Potash (GRO).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Arista Networks (ANET), Medtronic (MDT), and Baidu (BIDU). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Bumble (BMBL) and Iqiyi (IQ) after their reports.
  • All day - Notable investor events include the Jacobs Solutions' (J) Investor Day event.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Kraft Heinz (KHC), Hershey (HSY), General Mills (GIS), and Coca-Cola (KO).
  • All day - Shareholders with Discover Financial (DFS) and Capital One (COF) will vote on the pending mega-merger between the banking giants.
  • All day - The three-day CoinDesk Consensus Hong Kong event will begin. The crypto conference marks the first expansion of the Consensus event series beyond North America.
  • All day - The quiet period ends on Venture Global (VG) to free up analysts to issue ratings.
  • 10:20 a.m. San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly speaks and participates in a moderated discussion before the 2025 Conference for Community Bankers hosted by the American Bankers Association.

Wednesday - February 19

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Analog Devices (ADI), Toast (TOST), and HSBC (HSBC). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Carvana (CVNA) and Etsy (ETSY) after their reports.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from PepsiCo (PEP), Altria (MO), Molson Coors (TAP), and Philip Morris International (PM).
  • All day - Notable investor events include BrightView Holdings' (BV) Investor Day and Lam Research's (LRCX) Investor Day.
  • All day - Anbio Biotechnology (NNNN) is expected to start trading after the company prices its IPO.
  • 12:00 p.m. Apple (AAPL) will announce the launch of a new product at a launch event. The announcement is expected to be conducted through a press release and promotional materials rather than a live-streamed keynote. A fourth-generation iPhone SE is widely expected to be featured at the event, although there is speculation about a new MacBook Air, new iPad Air, or a surprise reveal.
  • 7:30 a.m. JetBlue Airways Corporation (JBLU) will participate in a fireside chat at the Barclays 42nd Annual Industrial Select Conference.
  • 11:35 a.m United Airlines (UAL) will present at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 2:00 p.m. The Federal Open Market Committee will issue minutes of its latest meeting.

Thursday - February 20

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Walmart (WMT), Alibaba (BABA), Booking Holdings (BKNG), MercadoLibre (MELI), VICI Properties (VICI), and Block (XYZ). Options trading implies a double-digit share price swing for Rivian Automotive (RIVN) and Unity Software (U) after their reports are released.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Procter & Gamble (PG), US Foods (USFD), Diageo (DEO), and Clorox (CLX).
  • 8:00 a.m Walmart (WMT) will hold its earnings conference call. The retail giant's business updates have impacted shares of Target (TGT), Costco (COST), Dollar General (DG), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and Kroger (KR) in the past. Major suppliers with a strong post-earnings trading correlation with Walmart include Reynolds Consumer Products (REYN), TreeHouse Foods (THS), and Flower Foods (FLO).
  • 8:30 a.m Mobileye Global (MBLY) will present at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 8:30 a.m The Philadelphia Federal Index report will be released.
  • 8:40 a.m Boeing (BA) CEO Kelly Ortberg will speak at the Barclays Industrial Select Conference.
  • 10:00 a.m Visa (V) will hold its Investor Day event.
  • 12:05 p.m St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak and participate in a moderated conversation on the U.S. economy and monetary policy before the Economic Club of New York.

Friday - February 21

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Vipshop (VIPS) and United States Cellular (USM).
  • All day - Global flash PMI reports will pour in.
  • All day - Sandisk (SDK) will start trading after being spun off from Western Digital (WDC). The business split will create two independent, publicly traded companies focused on different aspects of data storage technology.
  • All day - The CAGNY Conference will include presentations from Celsius Holdings (CELH) and Nestle (OTCPK:NSRGY).
  • All day - Apimeds Pharmaceuticals (APUS) is expected to start trading after the company prices its IPO.
  • 10:00 a.m. The Existing Home Sales report will be released by the National Association of Realtors.

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story.

data.title: Office Properties Income Trust outlines $140M debt maturity plan for 2026 amid challenging leasing conditions
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Office Properties Income Trust (NASDAQ:OPI) Q4 2024

Management View

  • The company addressed its debt maturities by completing $1.8 billion in secured financings in 2024 and reducing total debt principal by nearly $200 million. This included exchanging $488 million of new notes for $378 million of senior unsecured notes and using proceeds from the sale of 24 properties to repay the balance.
  • The portfolio consists of 128 properties with 17.8 million square feet and generates $428 million in annualized revenue, down from $513 million a year ago. Approximately 58% of revenues come from investment-grade tenants or their subsidiaries.
  • Leasing activity in Q4 included 13 leases totaling 359,000 square feet, with a notable 24.3% roll-up in rent. Highlights included a six-year renewal with AT&T for 191,000 square feet at a 25% rent increase.
  • GSA leases represent $70 million in annualized revenue, with 413,000 square feet in a soft term. The company forecasts at least one agency to terminate a lease in Q2 2025, representing $856,000 in annual revenue.
  • CFO Brian Donley stated, "For the fourth quarter, we reported normalized FFO of $20.9 million or $0.36 per share for the quarter, which came in $0.01 above our guidance range as a result of the impact and timing of our dispositions."

Outlook

  • The company projects normalized FFO of $0.08 to $0.10 per share for Q1 2025, reflecting lower NOI due to asset sales, tenant vacancies, and increased interest expense.
  • Same property cash basis NOI is expected to decrease 8% to 10% in Q1 2025 compared to Q1 2024, driven by tenant vacancies and increased free rent.
  • Upcoming lease expirations in 2025 account for 2 million square feet, representing $42 million or nearly 10% of annualized rental income. Known vacates are expected to account for $29.3 million of this revenue.
  • The company launched a debt exchange offer for up to $175 million of new senior guaranteed unsecured notes, prioritizing holders of 2026 notes.

Financial Results

  • Normalized FFO for Q4 2024 was $20.9 million or $0.36 per share, compared to $22.1 million or $0.43 per share in Q3 2024. The sequential decrease was attributed to higher interest expenses, partially offset by increased NOI.
  • Same property cash basis NOI grew 4.9% year-over-year, supported by lower operating expenses and asset sales.
  • The company completed $114.5 million in property sales during Q4 and has six properties under agreement for $55 million, with most expected to close by the end of Q2 2025.
  • Total liquidity stands at $113 million, with a projected cash burn of $60 million to $70 million in 2025, including capital expenditures.

Q&A

  • Bryan Maher, B. Riley Securities: Questioned the valuation of assets sold relative to carrying value. Yael Duffy, President and COO, explained that most assets sold were vacant or soon-to-be-vacant properties, often valued at a fraction of their carrying value.
  • Maher asked about leasing pipeline activity. Duffy shared that the pipeline includes 2 million square feet, with less than 20% in advanced stages, and emphasized stronger activity at multi-tenant properties versus single-tenant buildings.
  • Ronald Kamdem, Morgan Stanley: Inquired about the rationale behind the Q4 debt exchange. CFO Donley explained that the exchange aimed to reduce 2025 maturities and leverage remaining capacity from a previous debt issuance.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analyst sentiment was neutral to slightly negative, reflecting concerns about asset valuations, leasing challenges, and the company’s ability to address debt maturities.
  • Management sentiment remained cautious but constructive. Duffy described ongoing negotiations with 2025 noteholders as "very constructive" and highlighted efforts to mitigate liquidity pressures through asset sales and leasing activity.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar tone, emphasizing efforts to address debt maturities and improve operating metrics.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for Q1 2025 normalized FFO of $0.08 to $0.10 per share marks a decline from Q4 2024 guidance, reflecting increased tenant vacancies and interest expenses.
  • Leasing activity in Q4 2024 showed improvement, with a 24.3% roll-up in rent compared to lower growth rates in Q3 2024.
  • Liquidity decreased from $146 million in Q3 2024 to $113 million in Q4 2024, with the company projecting a cash burn of $60 million to $70 million in 2025.
  • Analysts in the previous quarter raised concerns about "substantial doubt" regarding the company’s going concern status, while Q4 discussions focused more on asset valuations and leasing prospects.

Risks and Concerns

  • Tenant vacancies and declining rents are expected to pressure NOI.
  • The GSA's government efficiency measures pose risks to key leases, with potential terminations affecting revenue streams.
  • Liquidity constraints limit the company’s ability to address upcoming debt maturities without asset sales or refinancing.

Final Takeaway

Office Properties Income Trust continues to navigate significant challenges, including tenant vacancies, declining revenues, and liquidity pressures. While Q4 leasing activity showed stronger rent roll-ups, the company remains focused on mitigating risks through asset sales, debt exchanges, and refinancing efforts. Upcoming lease expirations and the potential impact of GSA efficiency measures remain critical areas of concern, alongside projected cash burn and limited liquidity.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tiger Global's top Q4 moves include adding new PDD stake, exiting Toast, among others
data.description:
Chase Coleman's Tiger Global took fresh stakes in PDD Holdings (PDD), with 2.63M shares, Zscaler (ZS), with 1.45M shares, and CoStar Group (CSGP), with 3.25M shares, during the fourth quarter of 2024, according to the fund's latest 13F filing released on Friday.

The hedge fund, meanwhile, closed out its positions in Toast (TOST) and ZoomInfo Technologies (ZI).

Tiger boosted its holdings in Reddit (RDDT), to 2.03M shares from 500K shares, and Spotify Technology (SPOT), to 1.26M from 2.56K, the filing showed.

Its stakes were lowered in Nu Holdings (NU), to 8.88M shares from 18.4M, and UnitedHealth Group (UNH), to 438K from 2.37M.

data.title: Essent Group outlines $500M share repurchase and 11% dividend increase for 2025
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Essent Group Ltd. (NYSE:ESNT) Q4 2024

Management View

  • Mark Casale, Chairman and CEO, highlighted strong credit quality and resilience in housing and labor markets, which supported credit performance and investment income. He acknowledged that mortgage origination activity remains subdued but anticipates that home buying demand is postponed rather than diminished due to current affordability issues.

  • Essent reported net income of $168 million for Q4 2024, with diluted EPS of $1.58. For the full year, net income was $729 million, equating to $6.85 per diluted share. Return on average equity for 2024 stood at 14%.

  • As of December 31, 2024, the company’s U.S. mortgage insurance in force was $244 billion, reflecting a 2% increase year-over-year. Persistency was reported at 86%, slightly down from the previous quarter.

  • Essent announced an 11% increase in its quarterly dividend to $0.31 per share and a $500 million share repurchase authorization through the end of 2026.

  • David Weinstock, CFO, reported that cash and investments were $6.3 billion at year-end, with a new money yield in the fourth quarter exceeding 5%. The full-year 2024 investment yield was 3.7%, up from 3.5% in 2023. Net investment income for 2024 totaled $222 million, representing nearly a 20% increase from 2023.

Outlook

  • Casale stated that Essent's strong balance sheet and operating model position the company for a range of economic scenarios. He expects persistency to remain elevated near-term, supported by current mortgage rate levels.

  • Management projected that the average base premium rate for the U.S. mortgage insurance business will remain consistent with the Q4 2024 rate of 41 basis points through 2025.

  • Essent plans to continue its programmatic reinsurance strategy, having entered into two quota share transactions for 2025-2026 business.

Financial Results

  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 was $1.58 vs. analysts' estimate of $1.65. Revenue was not explicitly stated in the transcript, so no comparison to analysts' estimates is provided.

  • The provision for losses and loss adjustment expenses rose to $37.2 million in Q4 2024, compared to $29.8 million in Q3 2024, largely due to defaults from hurricanes. Approximately $8 million of the provision was attributed to hurricane-related defaults.

  • The default rate on the U.S. mortgage insurance portfolio increased to 2.27%, up from 1.95% in Q3 2024. However, management noted that the probability of claims from these defaults remains low.

  • Essent’s expense ratio for Q4 2024, excluding title operations, was 19.4%, reflecting disciplined cost management.

Q&A

  • Bose George, KBW, inquired about the outlook for the title business in 2025. Casale responded that results are expected to be similar to 2024 due to the business’s sensitivity to interest rates, but he reiterated a long-term positive outlook.

  • Terry Ma, Barclays, asked about the trajectory of defaults excluding hurricane-related effects. Casale noted that defaults are expected to rise slightly in 2025 due to portfolio seasoning and aging but emphasized the company’s strong credit quality.

  • Rick Shane, JP Morgan, raised concerns about the impact of rising insurance costs in high-risk areas. Casale assured that mortgage insurance policies require borrowers to maintain homeowners' insurance and that Essent’s exposure in high-cost areas remains limited.

  • Doug Harter, UBS, questioned the pacing of capital return amid muted industry growth. Casale indicated that Essent aims to balance capital returns with maintaining strategic optionality, leveraging its strong cash flows and PMIERs sufficiency.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about rising defaults, hurricane impacts, and subdued origination activity, reflecting a slightly cautious tone. Questions frequently probed claims exposure and credit performance, signaling skepticism about potential future risks.

  • Management maintained a confident tone in prepared remarks, emphasizing Essent’s strong balance sheet, capital generation, and disciplined operations. However, during Q&A, there were moments of defensiveness, particularly when addressing rising defaults and the title business outlook.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management’s tone showed slightly more caution in discussing credit performance and macroeconomic uncertainties.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Net income for Q4 2024 was $168 million, compared to $176 million in Q3 2024. Diluted EPS also declined quarter-over-quarter.

  • Persistency decreased slightly from 86.6% in Q3 2024 to 86% in Q4 2024. The default rate rose from 1.95% to 2.27%, reflecting hurricane-related impacts.

  • Management’s tone shifted slightly toward caution due to rising defaults and external challenges such as natural disasters.

Risks and Concerns

  • Rising defaults, particularly from aging vintages and hurricane-related impacts, pose a potential challenge. Management indicated that while defaults are increasing, the probability of claims remains low due to strong mark-to-market positions.

  • Persistency levels may decline further as mortgage rates stabilize, potentially impacting revenue growth.

  • The title business remains a drag on earnings in the near term, with no material improvement expected until interest rates decline significantly.

Final Takeaway

Essent Group delivered strong results in 2024, supported by robust credit quality and disciplined operations. While rising defaults and external risks like hurricanes warrant close monitoring, the company remains well-capitalized and positioned for long-term growth. With an 11% dividend increase and a $500 million share repurchase authorization, management demonstrated confidence in capital efficiency and shareholder returns.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: LiveOne targets $51M revenue for PodcastOne with expanded B2B partnerships
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: LiveOne, Inc. (NASDAQ:LVO) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Robert Ellin highlighted record revenues of $95 million for the first nine months of the fiscal year and $29 million for the quarter, driven by the performance of Slacker Radio and PodcastOne. Adjusted EBITDA for the nine months reached $14.1 million for the audio business.
  • Significant strides were made in B2B partnerships with five deals signed in the last 90 days, contributing over $44 million in revenues. Notable agreements include $25 million with a Fortune 500 media conglomerate and $16.5 million with Amazon.
  • LiveOne renewed its partnership with Tesla for the 12th consecutive year, transitioning from a white-label arrangement to a branded presence in every Tesla car. This shift is expected to unlock a multi-billion-dollar opportunity by leveraging data collection and direct subscriber engagement.
  • The company committed to a $12 million stock buyback program, with $6.2 million still available, reflecting confidence in its future.
  • CFO Aaron Sullivan noted LiveOne’s current cash position increased by $4 million to almost $11 million after repaying $3 million in debt and acquiring 900,000 shares of PodcastOne.

Outlook

  • LiveOne expects PodcastOne to generate $51 million in revenue for the fiscal year, coupled with positive EBITDA.
  • Management anticipates closing at least two more B2B partnerships by year-end, with a robust pipeline of over 70 deals in various stages of development.
  • CEO Ellin emphasized the potential to convert even 1–2% of users from these deals into paid subscribers, describing it as a multi-billion-dollar opportunity.
  • Future B2B partnerships are expected to involve companies with user bases ranging from 10 million to 1 billion, indicating substantial growth opportunities.

Financial Results

  • LiveOne reported $29 million in revenue for the quarter, contributing to $95 million for the first nine months. The audio business achieved $90 million in revenue during this period, a first in the company’s history.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for the nine months reached $14.1 million, with PodcastOne achieving positive EBITDA for the fiscal year.
  • LiveOne’s cash position improved to $11 million, driven by operational performance and strategic buybacks.
  • Tesla partnership changes include 800,000 new ad-supported subscribers since December, representing 40% of Tesla’s North American car base.

Q&A

  • Brian Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners: Questioned the anticipated average revenue per user (ARPU) and subscription model for Tesla users. CEO Ellin explained that pricing elasticity offers significant opportunities to raise prices, citing the industry trend of increasing subscription fees.
  • Kinstlinger asked about PodcastOne’s costs relative to revenue growth. CEO Ellin clarified that upfront costs and delayed advertiser payments impact margins initially but emphasized long-term profitability as new content generates revenue.
  • Sean McGowan, ROTH Capital Partners: Asked about monetization timelines for Tesla’s ad-supported subscribers. CEO Ellin stated that advertising takes 90–120 days to ramp up and expects substantial contributions within six to nine months.
  • McGowan inquired about the gross margin impact of new ad-supported users. Ellin responded that these costs have only partially reflected in the current quarter and noted future margin improvements as advertising revenue scales.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on monetization and margins, particularly for Tesla-related initiatives. Concerns were raised about potential interim pressure on gross margins.
  • Management maintained a confident tone, frequently highlighting transformative opportunities and emphasizing the long-term benefits of strategic shifts, particularly in Tesla and B2B partnerships.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the sentiment was more forward-looking, with management projecting substantial growth from recent initiatives.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The renewed Tesla partnership represents a notable strategic shift, transitioning from a guaranteed payment model to direct subscription and ad-supported monetization.
  • B2B partnerships expanded significantly this quarter, adding $44 million in revenue compared to initial efforts in prior quarters.
  • Management’s tone was more assertive, underscoring confidence in achieving multi-billion-dollar opportunities through user conversion and expanded partnerships.
  • Analysts focused more on Tesla’s monetization and margin dynamics than in the previous quarter, reflecting the partnership's evolving impact.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management acknowledged potential short-term revenue dips due to the transition in Tesla’s contract model but emphasized long-term growth opportunities.
  • Concerns about delayed monetization of ad-supported subscribers were addressed with plans for significant advertising revenue within 6–9 months.
  • Analysts questioned gross margin pressures from ad-supported user growth and the timing of revenue realization.

Final Takeaway

LiveOne, Inc. highlighted a transformative quarter marked by record revenues, a strategic shift in its Tesla partnership, and expanded B2B initiatives. The company’s efforts to leverage new partnerships and scale its ad-supported model are expected to unlock significant long-term growth opportunities, though short-term revenue and margin pressures remain focal points for analysts.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: xAI looking to raise $10B in new funding, would value AI startup at $75B: report
data.description:
Generative artificial intelligence startup xAI is looking to raise $10B in new funding that would value the Elon Musk-led company at $75B, Bloomberg reported.

Previous investors Sequoia Capital, Andreessen Horowitz, and Valor Equity Partners have been in discussions to participate in the new funding round, the news outlet added, citing a person familiar with the matter.

San Francisco-based xAI did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Maker of the Grok chatbot, xAI raised $6B in new funding late last year that valued the company at $50B. Some of that funding went towards xAI's massive data center in Memphis, where it will use 100,000 of Nvidia's (NVDA) H100 GPUs, Musk said in a post on X. The process to build it took 122 days.

In November, it was reported that the company was on track to generate $100M in annual revenue, though most of that was from Musk's other companies, including the social network X, previously known as Twitter.

One month prior, xAI released its first application programming interface, or API. Currently, the xAI API only has one model, known as “grok-beta.” This model is priced at $5M per million input tokens or $15M per million output tokens. It is unclear if the models are Grok-2 or Grok-2 mini, which were announced in August.

Last month, xAI, which competes with Microsoft (MSFT) backed OpenAI, Anthropic and others, released its official Grok app for iOS.

Musk said in July that he would bring the idea of Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) investing $5B into xAI to the electric vehicle maker's board of directors after the idea received nearly two-thirds of “yes” votes in an online poll.

News of the new funding round comes as a Musk-led group made an unsolicited offer to buy the non-profit entity that controls ChatGPT maker OpenAI for $97.4B. Included in the group is xAI, according to a letter of intent sent to OpenAI's board of directors.

Some have speculated that the offer for OpenAI is nothing more than a ploy from Musk (who is currently suing OpenAI in an attempt to stop its for-profit conversion) to slow down OpenAI's capital raising.

data.title: Vera plunges as Otsuka updates on rival therapy
data.description:
Shares of Vera Therapeutics (NASDAQ:VERA) lost ~20% on Friday to reach a new 52-week low, in a move attributed to a potential market launch of a rival kidney disease therapy from Otsuka Holdings (OTCPK:OTSKF) as a once-monthly regimen.

Citing the Tokyo-based firm’s earnings results on Friday, Evercore ISI analyst Liisa Bayko argued that Otsuka’s (OTCPK:OTSKY) sibeprenlimab, which rivals Vera’s (NASDAQ:VERA) atacicept in the IgA nephropathy space, could reach the market “a few months ahead.”

While both drugs are in late-stage development for the immune disorder, with key data updates expected in June-July, Bayko said that sibeprenlimab is designed for less frequent dosing than atacicept, which requires weekly dosing.

Despite potential volatility ahead of the readouts, “We continue to recommend the stock as the profile is greatly derisked & will benefit from first-ish mover advantage in IgAN, a field anxiously awaiting these disease-modifying treatments,” Bayko, with an Outperform rating on VERA, wrote.

Goldman Sachs also reaffirmed its Buy rating on VERA, arguing that the selloff is an overreaction as Otsuka’s (OTCPK:OTSKY) disclosure more or less aligned with future milestones in the IgAN space.

The analyst Paul Choi awaits a topline readout in Q2 2025 from VERA’s Phase 3 ORIGIN trial for atacicept in IgAN closely followed by the company’s future regulatory filings for the drug.
